theme: How a tech company kept their top 40 engineers from leaving — for $147 per employee per month

linkedin.text:
A 280-person Israeli tech company came to us in Q1 2025 with a problem.

Their top 40 engineers — the ones who built the core IP — had been getting LinkedIn messages from competitors with offers 25-40% higher. HR was already approving 2-3 retention bonuses per month. Burning $180k/quarter and still losing people.

The CFO asked: "What if we spent that same money on something they couldn't get from a competitor?"

We built a 12-month program:

→ A 4-day flagship trip to Prague (Q3 2025) — wives/husbands included for 12 hand-picked engineers
→ Two quarterly "micro-retreats" — 1.5 days each, smaller groups of 20-25
→ Quarterly partner dinners in TLV with their families
→ Reserved 6 spots at our 2026 Mallorca summer flagship for top performers

Total cost: $410k for 40 engineers across the year. That's $147/employee/month.

What happened in 12 months:
→ 0 of the 40 left (vs. industry attrition rate of ~18% for senior engineers in TLV)
→ Internal eNPS went from 4.2 → 8.1
→ 6 people referred friends — 3 hired (saving $90k in recruiting fees)
→ CFO calculated avoided replacement cost: ~$2.1M (using 1.5x salary replacement metric)

ROI: 5.1x in year one. The CFO now budgets these trips before retention bonuses.

Not all events are equal. The wrong one entertains. The right one signals: "You're not replaceable."

📩 alon@upe.co.il

Alon Ouaknine

#IncentiveTravel #CorporateEvents #UproductionEvents #MICE #EmployeeRetention #TechIndustry #HRStrategy #CaseStudy #B2B #CorporateTravel
